addressedFeedback.finding: Design accepted with no high or mid findings in supplied workflow input.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Focused TASK-008 plan follows accepted design and explicitly blocks release publication, tap mutation, TypeScript removal, and Swift production default.

addressedFeedback.finding: Self-review found TASK-008E was marked In Progress even though it depends on TASK-008A through TASK-008D, which are not started; this could mislead implementation ordering.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Changed TASK-008E status to Not Started, marked only plan/progress alignment checklist items complete, and updated the progress log to show no implementation tasks in progress.

addressedFeedback.finding: Implementation plan accepted with no high or mid findings in supplied workflow input.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Implemented TASK-008 readiness-only Swift packaging surfaces, blocked cutover gate manifest, deterministic Swift tests, documentation refreshes, and progress updates while keeping TypeScript/Bun as the production Homebrew fallback.

addressedFeedback.finding: Self-review found stale TASK-008 status rows in the parent module table and implementation-plan index after implementation moved the focused plan to In Review.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Updated parent plan TASK-008 module status, checked parent TASK-008 criteria, updated impl-plans/README.md to In Review, and logged the self-review status alignment.

addressedFeedback.finding: RIELA_VERSION was interpolated into Swift readiness work/archive paths before destructive rm -rf without validating path components or proving containment under RIELA_SWIFT_RELEASE_DIR.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Added semver-like version validation rejecting separators and parent traversal, absolute release-directory normalization, release-directory component validation, containment assertions for work/archive paths before dry-run output and writes, deterministic Swift coverage, and shell checks for unsafe RIELA_VERSION and release-directory traversal values.

addressedFeedback.finding: Generated Swift readiness .sha256 sidecars recorded absolute host archive paths, leaking machine-local paths and making checksum verification nonportable after relocation.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Changed checksum generation to run from the archive directory with the archive basename for shasum and sha256sum, added deterministic Swift coverage, and added shell verification from dist/swift-homebrew plus host-path rejection checks.
